title: Pleasingly Buttery Portuguese Pumpkin Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Portuguese stew made with pumpkin and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a cold winter's day. This dish is also ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly and can be served as a hangover cure.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor-clay-pot over medium heat.
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in the pot and add the chopped onion, carrot, and celery.
Cook the mirepoix mixture until it is softened and lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cubed pumpkin, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Stir to combine and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the stew, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the pumpkin is tender.
Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter until melted and serve hot.
